services.title: Java Development Services from Wise Accelerate

items.title: Spring Boot & Enterprise APIs
paragraphs: Spring Boot remains the default for production Java services: auto-configuration, actuator endpoints, and a vast ecosystem. Wise Accelerate structures apps in clear modules—web, domain, persistence—with validation, exception handling, and OpenAPI contracts so consumers integrate without guesswork.

Wise Accelerate configures Tomcat/Jetty/Undertow appropriately, uses WebClient or RestTemplate with timeouts and retries, and applies Spring Security for OAuth2/OIDC, method security, and CORS policies aligned to your identity provider.

items.title: Jakarta EE, Quarkus & Cloud-Native Java
paragraphs: Some enterprises standardize on Jakarta EE (formerly Java EE) or WebLogic/WebSphere patterns; Wise Accelerate modernizes where it pays off—extracting services, introducing containers, and clarifying transaction boundaries.
For fast startup and low memory on Kubernetes, Quarkus and Micronaut are options Wise Accelerate evaluates against your team’s skills and library needs—never as a default without trade-off analysis.

items.title: Persistence: JPA, JDBC & Distributed Data
paragraphs: Wise Accelerate uses Hibernate/JPA with explicit fetch strategies, second-level cache where justified, and Flyway or Liquibase migrations checked into CI. Read replicas and connection pools are tuned from metrics—not default pool sizes.
For high-throughput paths, Wise Accelerate may introduce jOOQ or native JDBC in narrow modules while keeping the domain model coherent—pragmatism over purity.

items.title: Microservices, Events & Resilience
paragraphs: Wise Accelerate designs services with clear APIs (REST, gRPC, or async events via Kafka/RabbitMQ). Resilience4j or Spring Cloud Circuit Breaker protects downstream calls; sagas or outbox patterns keep distributed transactions explainable.
Service discovery, configuration servers, or Kubernetes-native config are wired so environments stay reproducible and secrets never live in source control.

items.title: JVM Performance, Observability & Operations
paragraphs: Wise Accelerate tunes heap sizes, GC algorithms (G1, ZGC where appropriate), and thread pools using JVM flags and production metrics. Async and virtual threads (Project Loom) are adopted when profiling shows benefit—not by default.
Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), structured logging (JSON), and Micrometer metrics integrate with Prometheus, Grafana, or your APM so incidents are diagnosable across services.

items.title: Build, CI/CD & Long-Term Maintenance
paragraphs: Maven and Gradle builds are cached in CI; Wise Accelerate enforces reproducible builds and dependency audits. Container images use distroless or slim bases where security teams require minimal attack surface.
Java releases bring preview and LTS cadence; Wise Accelerate plans upgrades with compatibility tests and library support matrices so production does not lag years behind security fixes.

whyChoose.title: Why Choose Wise Accelerate for Java Development
whyChoose.imageAlt: Java and Spring developers at Wise Accelerate

features.title: Enterprise patterns that scale
features.body: Wise Accelerate builds Java systems with transactions, security, and operations as first-class concerns—patterns banks and insurers expect, expressed in maintainable code.

features.title: Kubernetes and hybrid ready
features.body: Whether you run on EKS, AKS, GKE, or on-prem clusters, Wise Accelerate packages Java services for health probes, graceful shutdown, and config externalization—so platform teams stay happy.

features.title: Senior JVM engineers
features.body: Wise Accelerate's Java developers understand classloaders, GC, and framework internals enough to fix production—not only tutorials. They document decisions so your team can own the system long-term.

keyThings.title: Key Things to Know About Java

sections.heading: 1. Stability and long-term support
sections.body: LTS releases and conservative enterprise adoption make Java a safe choice for systems that must run for years. Vendor support and a huge library ecosystem reduce “unknown unknowns” in large programs.

sections.heading: 2. Strong tooling and JVM maturity
sections.body: Debuggers, profilers, and GC advances make the JVM one of the most observable runtimes. Wise Accelerate uses that tooling to optimize after measurement, not folklore.

sections.heading: 3. Fit for large teams
sections.body: Static typing, established patterns, and modularization help many engineers work on one codebase—when boundaries and reviews are enforced. Wise Accelerate encodes those boundaries in code and architecture.

sections.heading: Backend services and integration
sections.body: REST and gRPC microservices, batch jobs, and enterprise integration hubs connecting ERP, CRM, and custom systems.

sections.heading: High-throughput and long-running systems
sections.body: Trading-adjacent stacks, telco, and logistics often standardize on Java for throughput and operational predictability under load.

sections.heading: Records, pattern matching, and Loom
sections.body: The language evolves quickly: records reduce boilerplate; virtual threads simplify concurrency for I/O-heavy workloads. Wise Accelerate adopts features when libraries and your baseline JDK support them.

sections.heading: Spring vs alternatives
sections.body: Spring Boot is the default for many greenfield services; Quarkus and Micronaut compete on startup and memory. Wise Accelerate picks based on team experience, vendor constraints, and measured cold-start requirements.

items.question: Is Java still relevant for new microservices?
items.answer: Yes—especially when teams value strong typing, mature libraries, and predictable operations at scale. Wise Accelerate compares Java with Go or Kotlin using your latency, hiring, and ecosystem constraints.

items.question: Spring Boot vs Quarkus—how do we choose?
items.answer: Spring Boot maximizes ecosystem and hiring pool; Quarkus can win on fast startup and lower memory for Kubernetes when your workload fits reactive or CDI-style programming. Wise Accelerate prototypes critical paths before committing.

items.question: How does Wise Accelerate handle JVM memory and GC tuning?
items.answer: Wise Accelerate starts with defaults and production metrics, then adjusts heap, GC, and thread pools based on evidence. Tuning without profiling is avoided—flags change when traces show benefit.

items.question: Can you work with our legacy monolith?
items.answer: Wise Accelerate uses strangler patterns, modularization inside the JVM, and clear APIs to peel off services incrementally—reducing big-bang risk while modernizing.

items.question: What about security (OWASP, dependencies)?
items.answer: Wise Accelerate integrates dependency scanning (e.g. OWASP Dependency-Check, Snyk), follows secure headers and validation practices, and patches CVEs on a defined cadence with regression tests.

items.question: Kotlin on the JVM vs pure Java?
items.answer: Kotlin interoperates with Java and can reduce boilerplate; adoption depends on team skills and Android alignment. Wise Accelerate can introduce Kotlin gradually in new modules if that matches your strategy.
